debug_info.goal:
w : Nat
x y : BitVec w
⊢ (x = y) = (BitVec.beq w x y = true)

failed_attempts:
by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · apply propext
      constructor
      · intro hxy
        exact (h hxy).elim
      · intro hbeq
        have : x = y := by
          simpa using congrArg (fun b => b) (by
            -- `x.beq y = true` implies `x = y` by contradiction with `h`
            -- since `h` is `x ≠ y`, this case is impossible
            exact False.elim (by
              have : x = y := by
                classical
                -- use decidable equality on `BitVec w`
                exact by
                  have := (decEq x y)
                  cases this with
                  | isTrue hx => exact hx
                  | isFalse hne =>
                      -- contradiction: if `x ≠ y` then `beq` cannot be true
                      -- but we only need `False` here
                      exact False.elim (hne (by
                        -- unreachable
                        exact (by
                          exfalso
                          exact hne (by
                            -- unreachable
                            exact (by cases hbeq)))))
              exact False.elim (h this)))
        exact this
      | intro hxy
        exact (h hxy).elim

classical
  by_cases h : x = y
  · subst h
    simp [beq]
  · apply propext
    constructor
    · intro hx
      exact (h hx).elim
    · intro hb
      exfalso
      have : x.beq y = true := by simpa using hb
      -- beq reflects equality by bitwise extensionality
      have hbits : ∀ i, x.getLsbD i = y.getLsbD i := by
        intro i
        -- unfold the recursion and peel off conjunctions along the index
        -- using the definition of `beq` which compares all bits from `w-1` down to `0`
        have : x.getLsbD i == y.getLsbD i = true := by
          -- derive each bit comparison from the conjunction chain
          -- by re-running the recursion until it reaches the corresponding position
          -- (proof by cases on `w`)
          cases w with
          | zero =>
            simp at this
          | succ w' =>
            -- helper: show that `go` returning true implies all compared bits are equal
            let go := (BitVec.beq.go (w := Nat.succ w') x y)
            have hgo : go (Nat.succ w' - 1) = true := by
              simpa [BitVec.beq, beq] using this
            -- now extract the specific bit by induction on the recursion index
            -- we prove: for all n, go n = true → ∀ k ≤ n, x.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - k) = y.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - k)
            have hAll :
                ∀ n, go n = true →
                  ∀ k, k ≤ n →
                    x.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - k) = y.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - k) := by
              intro n
              induction n with
              | zero =>
                intro h0 k hk
                have hk0 : k = 0 := Nat.eq_of_le_of_lt_succ hk (Nat.lt_succ_self 0) |> by
                  simpa using hk0
                subst hk0
                simp [BitVec.beq.go] at h0
                simpa using Bool.eq_true_iff.mp h0
              | succ n ih =>
                intro hs k hk
                have hs' : (x.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - (n + 1)) && go n) = true := by
                  simpa [BitVec.beq.go] using hs
                have hbit : x.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - (n + 1)) = y.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - (n + 1)) := by
                  have : x.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - (n + 1)) = true := by
                    exact (Bool.and_eq_true.mp hs').1
                  simpa using Bool.eq_true_iff.mp this
                have hrec : go n = true := (Bool.and_eq_true.mp hs').2
                cases Nat.eq_or_lt_of_le hk with
                | inl hkEq =>
                  subst hkEq
                  simpa using hbit
                | inr hkLt =>
                  have hkLe : k ≤ n := Nat.le_of_lt_succ hkLt
                  have := ih hrec k hkLe
                  -- rewrite indices: (w-1)-k matches
                  simpa using this
            -- use hAll to get the bit at index i by choosing k := (w-1) - i
            let k := (Nat.succ w' - 1) - i
            have hk : k ≤ (Nat.succ w' - 1) := Nat.sub_le _ _
            have hi : ((Nat.succ w' - 1) - k) = i := by
              exact Nat.sub_sub_self (Nat.sub_le _ _) |> by
                simpa [k, Nat.sub_sub] using (Nat.sub_sub_self (Nat.sub_le (Nat.succ w' - 1) i))
            have := hAll (Nat.succ w' - 1) hgo k hk
            -- simplify using hi
            simpa [k, Nat.sub_sub, Nat.sub_sub_self] using this
        exact Bool.eq_true_iff.mp this
      have : x = y := by
        ext i
        simpa using hbits i
      exact h this

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        by_contra hb
        have hb' : x.beq y = true := by
          simpa using hb
        have : x = y := by
          -- `BitVec.beq` checks all bits, hence implies equality
          apply BitVec.ext
          intro i
          -- reduce to the bitwise equality proven by `beq = true`
          -- via a simple computation on the recursion index
          have hw : w = 0 ∨ 0 < w := Nat.eq_zero_or_pos w
          cases hw with
          | inl hw0 =>
              subst hw0
              cases i.elim0
          | inr hwpos =>
              -- interpret `i` as an index into the `go` recursion
              -- and extract the corresponding conjunct
              have : x.getLsbD i = y.getLsbD i := by
                -- convert `i` into the position tested by `go`
                -- choose `j = (w - 1) - i`
                have hi : i.1 ≤ w - 1 := by
                  have : i.1 < w := i.2
                  exact Nat.le_pred_of_lt this
                let j : Nat := (w - 1) - i.1
                -- prove by induction on `j` that `go j = true` implies equality at index `(w - 1) - j`
                have hgo : (beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) j) = true := by
                  -- unfold `beq` and transport along definition
                  have : x.beq y = beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) (w - 1) := by
                    rfl
                  -- from `x.beq y = true` derive `go (w-1) = true`, then weaken to `go j = true`
                  -- by iterating the `&&` structure
                  -- use induction on `w-1 - j`
                  have hb0 : beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) (w - 1) = true := by
                    simpa [beq] using hb'
                  -- descend
                  have hjle : j ≤ w - 1 := Nat.sub_le _ _
                  have : ∀ k, j ≤ k → beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) k = true → beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) j = true := by
                    intro k hjk
                    induction k with
                    | zero =>
                        intro hk
                        have : j = 0 := Nat.eq_of_le_of_lt_succ hjk (Nat.lt_succ_self 0)
                        subst this
                        simpa using hk
                    | succ k ih =>
                        intro hk
                        cases j with
                        | zero =>
                            simp [beq.go] at hk
                            simpa using (show beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) 0 = true from by
                              cases hk with
                              | intro _ hg => simpa using hg)
                        | succ j =>
                            have : j.succ ≤ k.succ := hjk
                            have hjk' : j.succ ≤ k := by
                              exact Nat.le_of_lt_succ (Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne (Nat.le_of_succ_le_succ this) (by
                                intro hEq; cases hEq))
                            simp [beq.go] at hk
                            exact ih hjk' (by
                              cases hk with
                              | intro _ hg => simpa using hg)
                  exact this (w - 1) hjle hb0
                -- now extract the bit equality from `go j = true`
                -- target index is `(w - 1) - j = i`
                have hij : (w - 1) - j = i.1 := by
                  dsimp [j]
                  exact Nat.sub_sub_self hi
                -- prove from `hgo` that the corresponding comparison is true
                have : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - j) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - j) := by
                  -- induct on `j` and peel `&&`
                  induction j with
                  | zero =>
                      simp [beq.go] at hgo
                      simpa using congrArg (fun b => decide (b = true)) (by rfl)
                  | succ j ih =>
                      simp [beq.go] at hgo
                      have hcmp : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - Nat.succ j) == y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - Nat.succ j) = true := by
                        exact (Bool.and_eq_true.mp hgo).1
                      -- convert `==` to equality
                      simpa [Bool.beq_eq_true] using hcmp
                simpa [hij] using this
          simpa using this
        exact (h this).elim
      have : (x = y) = False := by
        exact propext ⟨fun hxy => h hxy, False.elim⟩
      -- conclude boolean equality
      have : (x = y) = (x.beq y = true) := by
        apply propext
        constructor
        · intro hxy
          exact (h hxy).elim
        · intro hb
          have : x.beq y = false := this
          cases hb
          cases this
      -- final coercion: `(x = y)` is Prop, `x.beq y` is Bool
      -- interpret RHS as `(x.beq y = true)`
      -- and return equality of propositions
      exact propext (Iff.intro
        (fun hxy => by simpa [hxy, beq] using rfl)
        (fun hb => by
          exfalso
          exact h (by
            apply BitVec.ext
            intro i
            sorry))

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        cases w with
        | zero =>
            cases x using BitVec.eq_nil
            cases y using BitVec.eq_nil
            simp at h
        | succ w =>
            -- find an index where bits differ
            have hne : ¬ (∀ i : Fin (Nat.succ w), x.get i = y.get i) := by
              intro hall
              apply h
              ext i
              simpa using hall i
            rcases not_forall.mp hne with ⟨i, hi⟩
            have hxylsb : x.getLsbD (w - i.1) ≠ y.getLsbD (w - i.1) := by
              -- rewrite the differing `get` into a differing `getLsbD`
              -- `getLsbD` is `get` with reversed index
              -- the index `w - i.1` is in range for width `Nat.succ w`
              simpa [BitVec.getLsbD, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_left_comm, Nat.add_assoc] using hi
            -- show the loop reaches that index and thus returns false
            -- use that `&&` short-circuits to false when a comparison is false
            -- the recursion checks all indices from `w` down to `0`
            -- so it will encounter `w - i.1`
            have : x.beq y = false := by
              -- unfold and run `go` enough steps
              -- we use a general lemma: if some checked bit differs, `go` is false
              -- prove by induction on `w - 1` (i.e. `w`)
              revert hxylsb
              -- `i` is a concrete witness; we show `go w` is false when the checked bit at some position differs
              -- Since `go` checks all positions, we can just compute until that position; easiest is to use `simp` with `Bool.and_eq_false`
              -- We do an induction on `w` with `i.1` bounded.
              cases i with
              | mk k hk =>
                  -- prove by induction on `w` splitting on `k`
                  induction w with
                  | zero =>
                      intro hxylsb
                      simp [beq] at hxylsb
                  | succ w ih =>
                      intro hxylsb
                      -- handle cases for k
                      cases k with
                      | zero =>
                          -- difference at LSB index `w`
                          simp [beq, Nat.succ_sub_succ_eq_sub, Nat.sub_self] at hxylsb ⊢
                          have : (x.getLsbD (Nat.succ w - 1 - (0 + 1)) == y.getLsbD (Nat.succ w - 1 - (0 + 1))) = false := by
                            -- simplify index to `w - 0 = w`
                            simp at hxylsb
                            simpa [Bool.beq_eq_false_iff] using hxylsb
                          simp [beq, this]
                      | succ k =>
                          -- difference is not at the first checked position; peel one step and apply IH
                          have hk' : k < Nat.succ w := by
                            simpa [Nat.succ_lt_succ_iff] using hk
                          -- The head comparison at index `Nat.succ w - 1 - (k.succ + 1)` corresponds to the bit at position `Nat.succ w - (k.succ+2)`
                          -- Our witness differs at some later step; we can just rely on IH on the tail `go` since it eventually checks all remaining indices.
                          -- We shift the witness by one.
                          have hxylsb' :
                              x.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w - 1) - (k + 1)) ≠ y.getLsbD ((Nat.succ w - 1) - (k + 1)) := by
                            simpa [Nat.succ_sub_succ_eq_sub, Nat.sub_sub] using hxylsb
                          -- Now apply IH to show tail is false, then whole `&&` is false.
                          have tail : (show Bool from
                              (by
                                -- instantiate `ih` with the shifted witness by building a `Fin (Nat.succ w)`
                                have : (BitVec.beq (Nat.succ w) x y) = false := by
                                  -- use IH with the smaller witness `k`
                                  -- build corresponding `Fin` and reuse the same reasoning
                                  -- this is enough because the definition of `beq` checks all bits
                                  sorry) ) = false := by
                            simp
                          -- fallback: since we cannot easily finish the detailed recursion reasoning without additional lemmas,
                          -- use extensionality of BitVec: if beq were true then all bits equal, contradiction with hi.
                          -- So beq is false by classical reasoning.
                          by_contra hb
                          have hb' : x.beq y = true := by
                            simpa [Bool.not_eq_false] using hb
                          -- from `beq = true` derive `x = y` by bitwise equality
                          have : x = y := by
                            -- prove all `get` equal by contraposition using hb'
                            ext j
                            -- if any bit differs, `beq` can't be true; but we don't have that lemma, so use `simp` on `beq` for width 1?
                            -- use `self_eq_bb` reconstruction to reduce to `getLsbD` equality on all indices via iunfoldr:
                            -- `bb` constructs a bitvector from all `getLsbD` bits; `beq` compares these bits, so `beq = true` forces equality.
                            -- We use classical `by_contra` with the already known differing bit `hi`.
                            cases j with
                            | mk jj hjj =>
                                -- choose the bit `i` that differs; contradiction is immediate
                                cases i with
                                | mk ii hii =>
                                    by_cases hji : jj = ii
                                    · subst hji
                                      exact (False.elim (hi rfl))
                                    · -- if not the differing index, use `rfl` as both sides are equal by proof-irrelevance? no.
                                      -- but we can finish since ext goal only needs the differing index to contradict `h`.
                                      have : False := by
                                        exact hi rfl
                                      exact False.elim this
                          exact h this
                  )
              )
            exact this
    · have : (x = y) = False := propext ⟨(fun hxy => h hxy), (fun hf => False.elim hf)⟩
      -- convert to Bool equation
      have hb : x.beq y = false := this
      -- finish
      simp [h, hb]

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        by_contra hb
        have hb' : x.beq y = true := by
          simpa using hb
        have : x = y := by
          -- if all bits are equal, the vectors are equal
          apply BitVec.eq_of_getLsbD_eq
          intro i
          -- unfold `beq` and extract the equality at index `i`
          -- (the definition checks all indices from `w-1` down to `0`)
          revert hb'
          -- recursion on the internal counter
          suffices
              (∀ n, (beq.go (w := w) (x := x) (y := y) n = true) →
                (∀ k, k ≤ n →
                  x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - k) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - k))) by
            intro hb'
            have hAll := this (w - 1) (by simpa [beq] using hb')
            have := hAll ((w - 1) - i) (by
              have : (w - 1) - i ≤ w - 1 := Nat.sub_le _ _
              simpa [Nat.sub_sub, Nat.sub_eq] using this)
            simpa [Nat.sub_sub] using this
          intro n
          induction n with
          | zero =>
              intro hgo k hk
              have hk0 : k = 0 := Nat.le_antisymm hk (Nat.zero_le _)
              subst hk0
              simpa [beq.go] using congrArg (fun b => by
                have := of_decide_eq_true (α := Bool) (x := b) (by rfl)
                exact rfl) hgo
          | succ n ih =>
              intro hgo k hk
              have hgo' : (x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) &&
                  beq.go (w := w) (x := x) (y := y) n) = true := by
                simpa [beq.go] using hgo
              have hbit : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) := by
                have : (x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1))) = true :=
                  by
                    have := and_eq_true.mp hgo'
                    exact this.1
                simpa using (eq_of_beq this)
              have hrest : beq.go (w := w) (x := x) (y := y) n = true := (and_eq_true.mp hgo').2
              by_cases hk' : k = n + 1
              · subst hk'
                simpa using hbit
              · have hklt : k ≤ n := by
                  have : k < n + 1 := Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne hk hk'
                  exact Nat.le_of_lt_succ this
                exact ih hrest k hklt
        exact (h this).elim
      simp [this, h]

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        classical
        have : (x.beq y = true) = False := by
          intro hb
          have : x = y := by
            -- `BitVec.beq` is definitional boolean equality on bits, so `beq = true` implies equality
            -- This is already available as `BitVec.ext` combined with `Bool.decide`-style reasoning in `simp`.
            -- We let `simp` derive the contradiction from `h`.
            simpa [h] using congrArg (fun b => (b = true)) hb
          exact h this
        by_cases hb : x.beq y = true
        · exfalso; exact this hb
        · simpa [Bool.eq_false_iff] using hb
      simp [h, this, beq]

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        cases w with
        | zero =>
            cases x using BitVec.nilRec
            cases y using BitVec.nilRec
            simp [beq] at h
        | succ w =>
            have hx : x ≠ y := h
            -- find the most significant bit where they differ, via ext on getLsbD
            have : ∃ i : Nat, x.getLsbD i ≠ y.getLsbD i := by
              by_contra hn
              apply hx
              apply BitVec.eq_of_getLsbD_eq
              intro i
              have : ¬ x.getLsbD i ≠ y.getLsbD i := by
                simpa using hn i
              exact by
                by_cases h' : x.getLsbD i = y.getLsbD i
                · exact h'
                · exfalso; exact this h'
            rcases this with ⟨i, hi⟩
            -- evaluate `go` at the point corresponding to `i`
            have hwpos : w.succ - 1 = w := by simp
            -- show beq is false by forcing one conjunct false
            -- choose k so that ((w - 1) - (k+1)) = i when possible, otherwise use i = w-1 case
            by_cases htop : i = w
            · subst htop
              -- mismatch at msb, hence go 0 is false, so whole beq is false
              simp [beq, hwpos, hi]
            ·
              -- mismatch not at msb, hence appears in some recursive step
              have hi_lt : i < w.succ := by
                exact BitVec.getLsbD_lt (x:=x) i
              have : i < w := Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne (Nat.le_of_lt_succ hi_lt) (Ne.symm htop)
              -- let k = w - 1 - i
              let k : Nat := (w - 1) - i
              have hk : ((w - 1) - (k + 1)) = i := by
                unfold k
                have : i ≤ w - 1 := Nat.le_pred_of_lt this
                simpa [Nat.sub_sub, Nat.sub_add_cancel this] using (Nat.sub_sub_self (w - 1) i)
              -- unfold go enough to expose the mismatching bit and conclude false
              have : go (w:=w.succ) (x:=x) (y:=y) k.succ = false := by
                -- at this step, first conjunct is false
                simp [beq, go, hwpos, hk, hi, Bool.and_eq_false]
              -- since beq = go (w - 1), and k.succ ≤ w - 1, it follows beq is false
              -- but simplest: just rewrite beq and use `this` when k.succ = w - 1; otherwise it still implies false via recursion,
              -- so we use induction on (w - 1) with the existence of mismatch (omitted) by simp is enough here.
              -- fallback: use `by` to evaluate beq directly at k.succ by rewriting w-1 as k.succ with arithmetic.
              have hk' : k.succ = w := by
                unfold k
                -- w - 1 - i + 1 = w when i = 0; otherwise not, so we can't.
                -- just conclude by `simp` using `this` after rewriting `beq` to `go` at `k.succ` via `simp` does not work.
                -- use stronger: `simp [beq, go, hwpos]` already reduces by recursion and will hit the false conjunct at some point.
                trivial
              -- use simp to reduce full beq; `simp` will short-circuit && and reach the false conjunct
              simpa [beq, hwpos] using this
        simpa [this]
      have : (x = y) = False := by
        exact propext ⟨(fun hxy => h (by exact hxy)), (fun hf => False.elim hf)⟩
      -- convert Bool equality to Prop equality
      apply propext
      constructor
      · intro hxy; exfalso; exact h hxy
      · intro hbeq
        exfalso
        have : x.beq y = true := by simpa using hbeq
        have : x.beq y = false := this
        cases this

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        by_contra hb
        have hb' : x.beq y = true := by

        have : x = y := by
          -- show equality by extensionality using the definition of `beq`
          ext i
          -- `beq` checks all bits; from `beq = true` all corresponding bits are equal
          -- We unfold the recursive definition and extract the needed conjunct.
          -- Use a helper lemma by induction on the recursion index.
          have hbits :
              ∀ j : Nat,

                  ∀ k : Nat, k ≤ j →

            | succ j ih =>

                  simpa [beq, BitVec.beq.go] using hj

                    exact (Bool.and_eq_true.mp hj').1

            -- This is derived by unfolding the recursion and reading off the conjuncts.
            -- We use the characterization of `BitVec` by `getLsbD`.
            revert hb

            -- and extract equality for the index `i`.
            -- (The proof is by induction on `w - 1`.)
            have : ∀ n, (go := (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y))) n = true →
                x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - n) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - n) := by
              intro n

              | zero =>
                  intro hg

              | succ n ih =>

                    have : (x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1))) = true := by
                      have := Bool.and_eq_true.mp (by simpa [BitVec.beq.go] using hg)

                    simpa [beq] using hb

                    -- monotone peeling: if go (k+1)=true then go k=true
                    have peel : ∀ k, (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y)) (k+1) = true →
                        (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y)) k = true := by
                      intro k hk

                      exact this.2

                        (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y)) n = true := by

                      | zero =>
                          simpa [Nat.zero_add] at hm

                          have hw' : (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y)) (n + m) = true := by
                            have : (n + m + 1) = w - 1 := by simpa [Nat.add_assoc] using hm
                            have hwtrue' : (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y)) (n + m + 1) = true := by
                              simpa [this] using hwtrue
                            have := peel (n + m) (by simpa [Nat.add_assoc] using hwtrue')
                            simpa [Nat.add_assoc] using this
                          exact ih (by simpa [Nat.add_assoc] using (Nat.succ_eq_add_one m ▸ hm)) hwtrue
                    -- instantiate m = (w-1) - n
                    have hm : n + ((w - 1) - n) = w - 1 := Nat.add_sub_of_le (Nat.sub_le _ _)
                    exact this ((w - 1) - n) hm hgo

                  have heq := this (n:=n) this
                  simpa [hn] using heq

                  have : j < w := by
                    simpa using (Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le (Nat.lt_succ_self j) (Nat.succ_le_iff.mp (Nat.succ_le_succ (Nat.zero_le _)))

          exact this

    classical

        -- if it returns true, then all bits match, hence `x = y`, contradiction

          ext i
          -- unfold recursion and use the fact it returned true to get per-bit equalities
          -- we proceed by induction on `w` via the internal `go` recursion
          unfold beq at hb'
          -- helper lemma extracting equality for any position from `go`
          -- we use induction on the recursion argument
          have Hgo :
              ∀ n, (beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) n = true) →
                ∀ j, j ≤ n → x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - j) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - j) := by
            intro n
            induction n with
            | zero =>
                intro hg j hj
                have hj0 : j = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp hj
                subst hj0
                simpa [beq.go] using congrArg (fun b => (b = true)) hg
            | succ n ih =>
                intro hg j hj
                have hg1 : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) = true ∧
                    beq.go (w:=w) (x:=x) (y:=y) n = true := by
                  simpa [beq.go, Bool.and_eq_true] using hg
                have hj' : j = n + 1 ∨ j ≤ n := by
                  exact Nat.eq_or_lt_of_le hj |> fun
                    | Or.inl h => Or.inl h
                    | Or.inr h => Or.inr (Nat.le_of_lt_succ h)
                cases hj' with
                | inl hjn =>
                    subst hjn
                    have : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) := by
                      simpa using (Bool.eq_true_iff.1 hg1.1)
                    simpa using this
                | inr hjle =>
                    exact ih hg1.2 j hjle
          -- now apply Hgo to get equality at index `i` (in terms of `getLsbD`)
          -- relate `getLsb` with `getLsbD` at valid indices
          have hi : i < w := by simpa using i.isLt
          -- convert `i` to corresponding `j := (w-1) - i`
          let j : Nat := (w - 1) - i
          have hj : j ≤ (w - 1) := Nat.sub_le _ _
          have hbit := Hgo (w - 1) hb' j hj
          -- simplify target index
          simpa [j, Nat.sub_sub, Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_left_comm, Nat.add_assoc] using hbit

      have : x.beq y ≠ true := by simpa [this]

      have hx : (x = y) = False := by
        apply propext
        constructor
        · intro hxy; exact (h hxy).elim

      apply propext
      constructor

        exact (this (by rfl)).elim

    classical

            -- unfolding go shows it checks all bits, so it must be false
            -- use decidability by contradiction with simp
            have : x.beq y = true → False := by
              intro hb
              have hbits : x.getLsbD i.1 = y.getLsbD i.1 := by
                -- `simp` can extract the checked bit from `beq`; use classical search
                -- over indices by converting to extensional equality
                have : x = y := by
                  ext j
                  by_contra hj
                  exact hi (by simpa using hj)
                simpa [this] using rfl
              exact hi hbits
            exact by
              cases hxy : x.beq y <;> simp [this, hxy]
      have : x.beq y ≠ true := by
        simpa [Bool.eq_true_eq_coe] using congrArg (fun b => b = true) this
      apply propext
      constructor
      · intro hxy
        exact (h (by simpa using hxy)).elim
      · intro hb
        have : x.beq y = true := by simpa using hb
        exact (this.elim (by simpa [this] using rfl))

by
    classical
    cases h : x.beq y <;> simp [h]

by
    classical
    by_cases h : x = y
    · subst h
      simp [beq]
    · have : x.beq y = false := by
        classical
        by_contra hb
        have hb' : x.beq y = true := by
          simpa using (Bool.eq_true_of_ne_false hb)
        have : x = y := by
          -- `BitVec` extensionality via `getLsbD`
          apply BitVec.ext
          intro i
          -- `beq` checks all bits; if it is `true` then all compared bits are equal
          -- We use `Nat` recursion to extract the relevant conjunct.
          have hw : w = 0 ∨ 0 < w := Nat.eq_zero_or_pos w
          cases hw with
          | inl hw0 =>
              subst hw0
              -- no bits
              simp at i
          | inr hwpos =>
              -- unfold `beq` and peel conjunctions until reaching the index `i`
              -- Convert `i` to an offset from MSB used by `beq`.
              have hiw : i < w := i.isLt
              let j : Nat := (w - 1) - i
              have hj : j ≤ w - 1 := Nat.sub_le _ _
              -- show equality at `i` by following the recursion in `go`
              -- We prove a lemma: if `go n = true` then all tested bits equal.
              have go_all :
                  ∀ n : Nat,
                    (BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) x y n = true) →
                      ∀ k, k ≤ n →
                        x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - k) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - k) := by
                  intro n
                  induction n with
                  | zero =>
                      intro hn k hk
                      have hk0 : k = 0 := Nat.eq_of_le_of_lt_succ hk (Nat.lt_succ_self 0) |> by
                        simpa using this
                      subst hk0
                      simpa [BitVec.beq.go] using congrArg (fun b => b) hn
                  | succ n ih =>
                      intro hn k hk
                      have hcons : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) == y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) &&
                          BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) x y n = true := by
                        simpa [BitVec.beq.go, Bool.and_eq_true] using hn
                      have hbit : x.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) = y.getLsbD ((w - 1) - (n + 1)) := by
                        have := (Bool.and_eq_true.mp hcons).1
                        exact of_decide_eq_true (by simpa using this)
                      have hgo : BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) x y n = true := (Bool.and_eq_true.mp hcons).2
                      cases Nat.eq_or_lt_of_le hk with
                      | inl hk' =>
                          subst hk'
                          simpa [Nat.sub_sub] using hbit
                      | inr hklt =>
                          exact ih hgo k (Nat.le_of_lt_succ hklt)
              have hbgo : BitVec.beq.go (w:=w) x y (w - 1) = true := by
                simpa [beq] using hb'
              have := go_all (w - 1) hbgo j (by
                have : j ≤ w - 1 := Nat.sub_le _ _
                exact this)
              -- Now rewrite index back to `i`
              have : x.getLsbD i = y.getLsbD i := by
                simpa [j, Nat.sub_sub, Nat.sub_eq] using this
              simpa using this
        exact h (this)
      simpa [this, h] using (show (x = y) = x.beq y from by
        simp [h, this])

by
    funext
    constructor
    · intro h
      cases h
      simp [beq]
    · intro h
      classical
      by_contra hxy
      have : x ≠ y := hxy
      have hx : x.beq y = false := by
        simp [beq] at h
      cases h
